title: Complete blood count
description: A broad blood test that measures red cells, white cells, hemoglobin, hematocrit, and platelets.
usage: Used to assess overall health, screen for anemia, infections, inflammation, bleeding issues, and blood disorders.
diagnostic_report_display: CBC W Auto Differential panel - Blood

title: Hemoglobin (Venipuncture)
description: Measures the concentration of hemoglobin in blood to assess anemia, polycythemia, and overall oxygen-carrying capacity.
usage: Used routinely in CBC to evaluate blood disorders, monitor treatment, and assess general health.
diagnostic_report_display: Hemoglobin [Mass/volume] in Blood

title: Hemoglobin (Finger Prick)
description: Measures the concentration of hemoglobin in blood to assess anemia, polycythemia, and overall oxygen-carrying capacity.
usage: Used routinely in CBC to evaluate blood disorders, monitor treatment, and assess general health.
diagnostic_report_display: Hemoglobin [Mass/volume] in Capillary blood

title: Total leucocyte count
description: Measures the number of white blood cells in the blood to assess immune activity.
usage: Used to check for infections, inflammation, immune disorders, or abnormal WBC levels.
diagnostic_report_display: Invalid

title: Differential leucocyte count
description: Breaks down the different types of white blood cells present in the blood.
usage: Used to evaluate infections, inflammation, allergies, and blood disorders by identifying changes in specific WBC types.
diagnostic_report_display: CBC W Auto Differential panel - Blood

title: Platelet count
description: Measures the number of platelets in the blood, which help in clotting.
usage: Used to evaluate bleeding tendencies, clotting disorders, bone marrow function, and monitor treatment affecting platelets.
diagnostic_report_display: Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count

title: MCH
description: Measures the average amount of hemoglobin present in each red blood cell.
usage: Used to classify types of anemia and assess red blood cell disorders.
diagnostic_report_display: MCH [Entitic mass] by Automated count

title: MCHC
description: Measures the average concentration of hemoglobin within red blood cells.
usage: Used to assess red cell hemoglobinization and help classify types of anemia
diagnostic_report_display: MCHC [Mass/volume] by Automated count

title: MCV
description: Measures the average size (volume) of red blood cells.
usage: Used to classify anemia as microcytic, normocytic, or macrocytic.
diagnostic_report_display: MCV [Entitic volume] by Automated count

title: MPV
description: Measurement of mean platelet volume
usage: Used to assess platelet production and help evaluate bleeding or clotting disorders.
diagnostic_report_display: Platelet mean volume [Entitic volume] in Blood by Automated count

title: RDW
description: Red cell distribution width determination
usage: Used to differentiate types of anemia and assess red blood cell size variability.
diagnostic_report_display: Erythrocyte distribution width [Ratio]

title: PDW
description: Measures the variation in platelet size distribution in the blood, indicating platelet heterogeneity.
usage: Used to differentiate between causes of thrombocytopenia, evaluate platelet production disorders, and assist in diagnosing reactive versus destructive thrombocytopenia.
diagnostic_report_display: Platelet distribution width [Entitic volume] in Blood by Automated count

title: Platelet count and morphology.
description: Measures the number of platelets in the blood and evaluates their size, shape, and structural characteristics on a peripheral smear.
usage: Used to assess bleeding risk, diagnose thrombocytopenia or thrombocytosis, evaluate platelet disorders, and monitor treatment affecting platelet production or function.
diagnostic_report_display: Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count

title: Absolute eosinophil count
description: Measures the total number of eosinophils in the blood.
usage: Used to evaluate allergic conditions, parasitic infections, drug reactions, and eosinophilic disorders.
diagnostic_report_display: Eosinophils [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count

title: Peripheral blood film
description: A microscopic examination of a stained blood smear to study the size, shape, and appearance of blood cells.
usage: Used to evaluate anemia types, infections, blood cell abnormalities, and to confirm findings from the CBC.
diagnostic_report_display: Blood smear finding [Identifier] in Blood

title: Erythrocyte sedimentation rate
description: Measures how quickly red blood cells settle in a tube, reflecting inflammation in the body.
usage: Used to detect and monitor inflammatory conditions, infections, autoimmune disorders, and some chronic diseases.
diagnostic_report_display: Erythrocyte sedimentation rate

title: Absolute neutrophil count
description: Measures the number of neutrophils in the blood.
usage: Used to assess infection risk and monitor neutropenia or bone marrow function.
diagnostic_report_display: Neutrophils [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count

title: Coagulation Profile (PT ,INR, aPTT)
description: A panel of tests that evaluates the blood clotting mechanism, typically including PT, INR, aPTT, and fibrinogen levels.
usage: Used to assess bleeding disorders, monitor anticoagulant therapy, evaluate liver function related to clotting factor production, and preoperative screening for surgical procedures.
diagnostic_report_display: PT and aPTT panel - Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay

title: Routine Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) staining
description: A standard histopathological staining technique that stains cell nuclei blue-purple (hematoxylin) and cytoplasm/extracellular matrix pink (eosin) on tissue sections.
usage: Used for routine microscopic examination of tissue biopsies to identify cellular structures, diagnose diseases including cancers, infections, and inflammatory conditions.
diagnostic_report_display: Microscopic observation [Identifier] in Tissue by Hematoxylin and eosin stain

title: Prothrombin Time (PT) and INR
description: Measures how long blood takes to clot and standardizes clotting time using INR.
usage: Used to assess clotting function, monitor warfarin therapy, and detect bleeding or liver disorders.
diagnostic_report_display: Invalid

title: Activated partial thromboplastin time
description: Measures the time it takes for blood to clot through the intrinsic and common coagulation pathways.
usage: Used to evaluate bleeding disorders, monitor heparin therapy, and assess overall coagulation status.
diagnostic_report_display: Activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT) in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay -- after addition of protein C activator/Activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT)

title: Bleeding time
description: Measures the time taken for bleeding to stop after a standardized skin puncture.
usage: Used to screen for platelet function defects, clotting disorders, and to assess basic hemostasis.
diagnostic_report_display: Bleeding time

title: Clotting time
description: Measures the time taken for blood to clot after it is drawn into a test tube.
usage: Used to assess the intrinsic coagulation pathway and overall clotting function
diagnostic_report_display: Clotting time of Capillary blood by Sukharev method

title: Blood group (ABO)
description: Determines the ABO blood group (A, B, AB, or O) of an individual.

title: Blood group (Rh typing)
description: Determines the presence or absence of the Rh (D) antigen on red blood cells.

title: Blood cross matching
description: Checks compatibility between donor blood and recipient blood before transfusion.
usage: Used to prevent transfusion reactions by ensuring the donor’s red cells and recipient’s serum are compatible.

title: Test for Dengue
description: Detects dengue virus infection using antigen or antibody-based methods.
usage: Used to diagnose acute dengue infection, monitor disease progression, and guide clinical management.
diagnostic_report_display: Gas panel - Arterial blood

title: Throat swab for Diphtheria
description: A microbiological culture test performed on a throat swab specimen to identify Corynebacterium diphtheriae, the causative organism of diphtheria.
usage: Used in suspected cases of diphtheria to confirm infection and guide treatment and public health measures.
diagnostic_report_display: Lactate dehydrogenase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Lactate to pyruvate reaction

title: Gram staining for clinical specimen
description: Detects Corynebacterium diphtheriae from a throat specimen to diagnose diphtheria.
usage: Used to guide early diagnosis of bacterial infections and support antibiotic selection before culture results.
diagnostic_report_display: Dengue virus Ab.IgG and IgM and NS1 Ag panel - Serum or Plasma

title: Stool for hanging drop for Vibrio Cholera
description: A rapid microscopic test to detect motile Vibrio cholerae in fresh stool.
usage: Used in suspected cholera cases for quick bedside diagnosis before culture confirmation.
code_display: Stool for hanging drop for Vibrio Cholera
diagnostic_report_display: Diphtheria identified in Throat by Organism specific culture

title: Typhoid test (IgM)- venipuncture
description: Detects IgM antibodies specific to Salmonella typhi, indicating recent typhoid infection.
usage: Used to diagnose acute typhoid fever, especially in early stages when cultures may be negative.
diagnostic_report_display: Microscopic observation [Identifier] in Specimen by Gram stain

title: Typhoid test (IgM)- Finger prick
description: Detects IgM antibodies specific to Salmonella typhi, indicating recent typhoid infection.
usage: Used to diagnose acute typhoid fever, especially in early stages when cultures may be negative.
diagnostic_report_display: Invalid

title: Smear for leprosy
description: Detects acid-fast Mycobacterium leprae in skin or nasal smears to support the diagnosis of leprosy.
usage: Used to confirm multibacillary disease, assess bacterial load, and monitor treatment response.
diagnostic_report_display: Salmonella typhi O Ab [Presence] in Serum

title: IgM for Measles
description: Detects IgM antibodies produced in response to recent measles infection.
usage: Used to confirm acute measles in patients with fever, rash, and respiratory symptoms, especially during outbreaks.
diagnostic_report_display: Salmonella typhi O Ab [Presence] in Serum

title: Test for Leptospirosis
description: Detects antibodies or antigens related to Leptospira infection.
usage: Used to diagnose leptospirosis in patients with fever, jaundice, muscle pain, or exposure to contaminated water.
diagnostic_report_display: Microscopic observation [Identifier] in Specimen by Acid fast stain

title: Test for Chikungunya
description: Detects IgM antibodies or viral components associated with Chikungunya virus infection.
usage: Used to diagnose suspected Chikungunya in patients with fever, severe joint pain, or rash.
diagnostic_report_display: Measles virus IgM Ab [Presence] in Serum

title: FNAC - Routine MGG
description: Fine Needle Aspiration Cytology with May-Grünwald-Giemsa staining, where cells are aspirated from a mass or lesion and stained for microscopic examination.
usage: Used for initial cytological evaluation of superficial lumps and masses to differentiate benign from malignant lesions and guide further management.
diagnostic_report_display: Leptospira interrogans IgM Ab [Presence] in Serum by Immunoassay

title: FNAC - Special stain when necessary
description: Fine Needle Aspiration Cytology with additional special staining techniques (e.g., PAS, Alcian Blue, ZN stain) applied as needed to identify specific cellular components or organisms.
usage: Used when routine staining is inconclusive, to identify specific organisms (e.g., acid-fast bacilli), substances (e.g., mucin, glycogen), or to further characterize lesions for accurate diagnosis.
diagnostic_report_display: Chikungunya virus Ab [Presence] in Serum

title: FNAC – USG guided
description: Fine Needle Aspiration Cytology performed under real-time ultrasound guidance to accurately target deep-seated or non-palpable lesions for cell aspiration.
usage: Used for cytological evaluation of deep or difficult-to-access masses in organs such as the thyroid, liver, lymph nodes, and other soft tissues to obtain accurate diagnostic samples.
diagnostic_report_display: Invalid

title: Bronchioalveolar lavage fluid for cytology
description: A cytological examination of fluid collected by washing (lavaging) the bronchial and alveolar spaces of the lung during bronchoscopy.
usage: Used to diagnose pulmonary infections, interstitial lung diseases, pulmonary hemorrhage, and malignancies by evaluating the cellular composition of the lavage fluid.
diagnostic_report_display: Invalid

title: Widal test
description: Serological agglutination test detecting antibodies against Salmonella Typhi and Paratyphi antigens. Used in the supportive diagnosis of enteric fever.
usage: Used to support the diagnosis of enteric (typhoid) fever by detecting antibodies against Salmonella O and H antigens.
diagnostic_report_display: Invalid
specimen_names: ZN Stain for Extra pulmonary sample
observation_names: ZN Stain for Extra pulmonary sample

title: Dengue NS1
description: An immunoassay test that detects the Non-Structural protein 1 (NS1) antigen of the dengue virus in the blood during early stages of infection.
usage: Used for early diagnosis of dengue fever, typically within the first 1-5 days of symptom onset, before antibody responses develop.
diagnostic_report_display: Cytology report of Bronchoalveolar lavage Cyto stain

title: Dengue IgM Antibody
description: An immunoassay test that detects IgM antibodies produced by the immune system in response to a current or recent dengue virus infection.
usage: Used to diagnose acute or recent dengue infection, typically detectable from day 4-5 of illness onset, and to support surveillance and epidemiological monitoring.
diagnostic_report_display: Salmonella typhi H Ab [Titer] in Serum by Agglutination

title: Dengue IgG Antibody
description: An immunoassay test that detects IgG antibodies against the dengue virus, which indicate a past or secondary dengue infection.
usage: Used to identify past dengue exposure, differentiate between primary and secondary dengue infections, and assess population-level dengue seroprevalence.
diagnostic_report_display: Dengue virus NS1 Ag [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay

title: HBsAg (Quantitative)
description: A quantitative immunoassay that measures the concentration of Hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg) in the serum.
usage: Used to confirm Hepatitis B virus infection, monitor disease progression, assess treatment response to antiviral therapy, and predict outcomes in chronic Hepatitis B patients.
diagnostic_report_display: Dengue virus IgM Ab [Presence] in Serum

title: Rapid malaria test for PF
description: A rapid immunochromatographic test that specifically detects Plasmodium falciparum antigens (primarily HRP-2) in the blood.
usage: Used for rapid and specific diagnosis of Plasmodium falciparum malaria, the most virulent species, to enable prompt initiation of species-appropriate antimalarial treatment.
diagnostic_report_display: Dengue virus IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum

title: Rapid malaria test for PV
description: Detects P. vivax–specific antigens in blood. Used for rapid malaria diagnosis.
usage: Used for rapid diagnosis of Plasmodium vivax malaria in febrile patients.
diagnostic_report_display: Hepatitis B virus surface Ag [Units/volume] in Serum

observation_names: Early Infant Diagnostic test for HIV - Qualitative HIV-1 DNA PCR (ICTC) (Venipuncture)

title: ZN Stain for Extra pulmonary sample
description: Ziehl-Neelsen acid-fast staining technique applied to extra-pulmonary specimens (e.g., tissue, body fluids, CSF, lymph node aspirate) to detect acid-fast bacilli (AFB).
usage: Used to diagnose extra-pulmonary tuberculosis and other mycobacterial infections in sites outside the lungs, such as lymph nodes, bones, joints, abdomen, and meninges.
code_display: ZN Stain for Extra pulmonary sample
diagnostic_report_display: Plasmodium falciparum Ag [Presence] in Blood by Rapid immunoassay

observation_names: Early Infant Diagnostic test for HIV - Qualitative HIV-1 DNA PCR (ICTC) (Heel Prick Test)

title: Stool R/E and parasites
description: A routine microscopic examination of stool specimen to evaluate its physical, chemical, and microscopic characteristics including the presence of ova, cysts, and parasites.
usage: Used to diagnose gastrointestinal infections, identify parasitic infestations, detect occult blood, assess fat malabsorption, and evaluate causes of diarrhea and abdominal symptoms.
diagnostic_report_display: Plasmodium sp Ag [Identifier] in Blood by Rapid immunoassay
specimen_names: Early Infant Diagnostic test for HIV - Qualitative HIV-1 DNA PCR (ICTC)(Dried blood spot specimen)
observation_names: Early Infant Diagnostic test for HIV - Qualitative HIV-1 DNA PCR (ICTC)(Dried blood spot specimen)

title: Sickling Test for Sickle cell anemia*
description: A screening blood test that induces red blood cell sickling under low-oxygen conditions to detect the presence of sickle hemoglobin.
usage: Used for preliminary screening of sickle cell anemia and sickle cell trait.
code_display: Sickling Test for Sickle cell anemia*
diagnostic_report_display: Microscopic observation [Identifier] in Specimen by Acid fast stain.Ziehl-Neelsen

title: S. Total Iron binding capacity
description: Measures the maximum amount of iron that can be bound by transferrin in the blood.
usage: Used to assess iron deficiency anemia and disorders of iron metabolism.
